assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions dev Rapid channel clusters and prod Stable channel clusters, with specific guidance to validate on dev clusters first

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Includes Autopilot-specific sections mentioning resource requests and security policies, though doesn't explicitly mention no SSH/node management limitations

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Mentions API deprecations review but doesn't provide specific kubectl commands to check for deprecated APIs

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Post-upgrade checklist includes 'Resource requests: Confirm workloads are getting appropriate resources' as an Autopilot-specific check

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Does not mention PodDisruptionBudgets, bare pods, or graceful shutdown configurations in the pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Provides general troubleshooting commands but no specific commands to verify the Kubernetes version after upgrade

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Includes comprehensive workload health checks including pod status, service connectivity, and application testing

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends validating on dev clusters and testing critical workloads on dev before prod upgrade

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE release schedule or advance notice beyond the standard notifications
Comprehensive monitoring section including resource utilization, application metrics, error rates, latency metrics, and log aggregation
